sections.title: Work
paragraphs: I currently work as an AI Research Engineer at Codeway Studios, where I focus on developing cutting-edge audio-visual generative models. This role allows me to explore the fascinating intersection of artificial intelligence and multimedia content generation.

Prior to this, I gained valuable experience at the Autonomous Vision Group, where I worked on model-based policy learning for urban driving, and at KUIS AI Laboratory, where I conducted research in artificial intelligence applications.

sections.title: Education
paragraphs: I took my BSc. degree from Middle East Technical University (METU) in Aerospace Engineering. I specialized in control, dynamic modelling, and simulation during my undergraduate studies.

Later, I conducted my graduate studies in Computational Science and Engineering department at Koc University. In Koc University, I am also awarded with Koc University Is Bank (KUIS) Artificial Intelligence Fellowship.

Initially, I worked in Robotics and Mechatronics Laboratory (RML) with Prof. Dr. Cagatay Basdogan. My work in RML mainly focused on the generation of proper signals to convey tactile feeling to high-end consumer electronics with touch screen (i.e., surface haptics).

Eventually, I worked as a computer vision researcher supervised by Asst. Prof. Fatma Guney in Autonomous Vision Group (AVG) and co-supervised by Asst. Prof. Baris Akgun. I worked on the model-based policy learning for urban driving.
I was especially interested in generating a framework that allows reasoning about the optimal action by predicting the future (i.e., world models).

footer.aboutText: Engineer, programmer, researcher, and a dreamer passionate about technology and innovation.

items.title: Maybe One Day...
items.subtitle: Philosophical exploration of reality through AI entities
items.description: Exchange of ideas and questions that push the boundaries of our understanding

items.title: Paper Digest
items.subtitle: AI-powered research companion
items.description: Automated paper retrieval and natural language summarization pipelines

items.title: CARLA Environment
items.subtitle: A customizable environment for autonomous driving
items.description: A customizable environment for autonomous driving using CARLA simulator.

items.title: Actor-Critic Model Predictive Control
items.subtitle: An interplay between RL and MPC
items.description: A model predictive control framework that uses Actor-Critic model to predict the future state of the system.

items.title: Haptics
items.subtitle: Electromechanical device control unit
items.description: Projects focused on controlling experimental setups in the Robotics and Mechatronics Laboratory. Includes Surface Haptics Setup control and TacTX - a framework for DAQ device control in MATLAB.

items.title: Deep Learning in Julia
items.subtitle: Various ML-related projects in Julia
items.description: A collection of deep learning implementations in Julia using Knet framework, including HapticFCN, FCN library, and data handling utilities.

items.title: TensorLib
items.subtitle: A minimalist tensor library written in C++
items.description: A tensor library for basic operations, created for a deep-learning-from-scratch project. Supports arbitrary dimensions and various mathematical operations.

items.title: Computational Physics
items.subtitle: A general look on computational physics
items.description: Collection of computational physics projects, including analysis of White Dwarf data and various physics simulations.

items.title: Robot Motion Planning
items.subtitle: Motion planning for 2-linked robotic arm
items.description: Implementation of a motion planning algorithm using Potential Field Method for a 2-linked robotic arm.

items.title: Swarm Algorithm
items.subtitle: A simple swarm algorithm simulation
items.description: Light-weight implementation of swarm intelligence algorithms in C++.

items.description: Working as an AI research Engineer at Codeway Studios. Working on audio-visual generative models.

items.description: Working as a computer vision researcher at Autonomous Vision Group. Working on the model-based policy learning for urban driving.
